c.d: The maximum number of contacts or recipients that can be messaged simultaneously or within a campaign
Types of messages supported, such as SMS, MMS, group chat, multimedia sharing, and direct messages
User interface simplicity and ease of setting up group or broadcast messages, including scheduling and automation capabilities
Support for integration with productivity tools, business workflows, and app ecosystems (e.g., Google Workspace, Microsoft 365)
c.s: Lark, Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Chat

c.d: Availability of real-time analytics, delivery reports, and engagement metrics to monitor message campaign performance
Measures like end-to-end encryption, decentralized communication, and data protection for message content
Availability on multiple platforms such as iOS, Android, Windows, and Mac to reach diverse users
Overall cost including free tiers, subscription plans, and per-message fees for broadcasting to multiple contacts
